media_title: Peep and the Big Wide World

characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.media_title: Peep and the Big Wide World
characters.description: Chromatic Arrangement: We have Chirp as the Red bird, Peep as the Yellow baby chick and Quack as the Indigo (close to blue) duck. Nice, Mean, and In-Between: Downplayed. Peep is the "nice" due to being a baby chick with no short temper as the most innocent of the trio. Quack is the "mean" being often snooty and rude at times, butstill loves his two friends dearly. Chirp is the "in-between"; while not mean at all, she alsodoesn't put up with Quack's shennanigans.

characters.name: Peep
characters.media_title: Peep and the Big Wide World
characters.description: Armless Biped: He isn't drawn with wings. Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny!: Justified since he's a baby. Children Are Innocent: He's the youngest and most naive of the cast. Cheerful Child: There's not a lot that can get him down. Cloudcuckoo Lander: He can be spacey. Nowhere near the level of Quack and/or Beaver Boy, though.

characters.name: Chirp
characters.media_title: Peep and the Big Wide World
characters.description: Berserk Button: She really doesn't appreciate anyone being a jerk to her or Peep, and is always ready to stand up to them. Beware the Nice Ones: You don't want to get on her bad side. Butt-Monkey: Chirp keeps on falling, getting dropped on and getting wet, partly from her attempts at flying or her friends' antics. Deadpan Snarker: She has some of the best one-liners of the series. The Determinator: She will always keep trying to fly.

characters.name: Quack
characters.media_title: Peep and the Big Wide World
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: He wears a sailor hat and is the only one of the main characters to wear any clothes at all. Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: An indigo duck. Armless Biped: Like Peep, he has no wings and uses his feet almost as if they wereFeather Fingers. Attention Whore: At one point he throws a party just because he wants attention. Blatant Lies: In the episode when he tells Chirp and Peep a story, he said the ducks in his family were many colors, and that ducks are considered sacred. It's pretty obvious he was fibbing about the second part.

characters.name: Beaver Boy
characters.media_title: Peep and the Big Wide World
characters.description: The Ditz: Even by this show's standards. The narrator lampshades this in one episode. Extreme Omnivore: He likes to eat sticks. Fat Idiot: Size-wise, when he's with the main trio, he's the fat one and he's not really bright, being more naive than Peep is. Furry Reminder: He's usually chewing on trees or anything wooden, which is probably because his teeth won't stop growing. Kindhearted Simpleton: Not the brightest, but is well-meaning, even more so thanQuack.

characters.name: Newton
characters.media_title: Peep and the Big Wide World
characters.description: Early-Installment Weirdness: His short film equivalent was French and went by the name of "Pierre". He was also less slow and spacey and more of an agoraphobe. Heavy Sleeper: He spends a lot of his time sleeping. It's actually easier to count how many times he was awake. Nice Guy:He's friendly, if a bit slow. Punny Name: He's named after Isaac Newton and lives under a crabapple tree that constantly drops apples on his shell. Sturdy and Steady Turtles: He's a turtle (or tortoise, rather), so he's slow.

characters.name: Tom
characters.media_title: Peep and the Big Wide World
characters.description: Big Bad: The closest thing to one in the series, anyway. Whenever he shows up, you know he'll be causing all of the trouble for the birds in that episode. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: SeeManipulative Bastard. Cats Are Mean: Manipulative, troublemaking, wants to eat the birds, etc. Cute Is Evil: Downplayed on the evil part, but is more than willing to cause problems for those he does not like. Cute Kitten: But don't be fooled.

characters.name: Nellie
characters.media_title: Peep and the Big Wide World
characters.description: Big Friendly Dog: Big compared to the birds, and very friendly. Dogs Are Dumb: Inverted; she's one of the smartest characters in the show. Early Installment Character-Design Difference: Her design changed the most from the original short film; she was considerably shaggier and had a raspy, southern accent (she called herself "Nellie the Country Dog"). In the actual series, she looks more like a golden retriever and has a softer, smoother voice. Heavy Sleeper: When she does appear, she's usually napping, however, she doesn't sleep as much as Newton does. Team Mom: Acts fairly motherly to the three birds.

characters.name: Hoot
characters.media_title: Peep and the Big Wide World
characters.description: Dark Is Not Evil: She might look menacing, but has a good heart.

characters.name: Splendid Bird from Paradise (Whistle)
characters.media_title: Peep and the Big Wide World
characters.description: Artistic License – Animal Care: According to her origin story flashback, she was captured and sold as a pet, which doesn't happen (in modern times anyway, as that's illegal), as birds kept as pets are captive-born and, as a pet, she was kept in a rather small cage without toys, which is a big no-no, as parrots (or parakeets) do best in big cages and need things to do because they'll pluck their feathers otherwise. Also, pet birds tend to have their wings clipped (or trained), so they won't fly off. Gratuitous Spanish: Adds Spanish to her sentences. Overly Long Name: She wanted to be called "Splendid Bird from Paradise" but this gets shortened to "Splendid Bird" Where the Hell Is Springfield?: Where she came from, though, since she speaks Spanish, we can guess she was caught and shipped from Central or South America (the show is implied to take place in North America).

characters.name: Humans
characters.media_title: Peep and the Big Wide World
characters.description: The Faceless: The most we see of them are their hands and feet. The closest we get to seeing them full is where we see a dad and his daughter's silhouettes in a pet store (see?◊).
